name: thumper
description: Brings the worm to the surface. Local supply-chain watchdog: OSV malware checks, integrity baselines for your configs AND installed extensions, npm registry/.npmrc tamper detection, dependency cooldown, and install-script auditing. No account, no telemetry.

commands.title: Thumper: Check dependencies against OSV (malware database)
Thumper: Accept current configs as baseline

thumper.suspectScopes.description: Extra npm scopes to flag (the bundled IOC baseline is always included).

thumper.suspectPackages.description: Extra package names to flag (bundled baseline always included).

thumper.scanOnStartup.description: Run the persistence + integrity scan when a workspace opens.

thumper.watchTerminal.description: Warn on risky npm/pnpm/yarn installs in the terminal (best-effort, after the command starts).

thumper.checkHardening.description: Check .npmrc floor + lockfile presence on open.

thumper.configIntegrity.description: Baseline protected config files (Claude settings, tasks.json, .npmrc, package.json, VS Code settings) and alert on ANY change, suspicious-looking or not.

thumper.extensionIntegrity.description: Baseline installed VS Code extensions (version + hash of their entry files) and alert on tampering: changed code without a version bump is treated as HIGH; normal version updates are noted and re-baselined.

thumper.extensionRecheckMinutes.description: How often (minutes) to re-check installed extensions while the editor is open, since marketplace auto-updates can land mid-session. 0 disables periodic re-checks.

thumper.minDependencyAgeDays.description: Cooldown: flag lockfile-resolved versions published more recently than this many days ago. 0 disables.

thumper.maxAgeChecks.description: Max packages to query per age check (registry packuments are large; results are cached 24h).

thumper.ageCheckOnStartup.description: Also run the dependency age check automatically on open (network calls to registry.npmjs.org).

thumper.osvCheck.description: Batch-check lockfile dependencies against the OSV malicious-package database on open (api.osv.dev, no key, cached 24h).
